name: Space Fleets — Rebel Vanguard
description: 10 vehicles — Scrappy freedom fighters and their ragtag fleet. Nimble interceptors, converted freighters, and stolen capital ships held together with field repairs and stubborn courage.

vehicles.name: Nimble Interceptor
vehicles.desc: A compact single-seat starfighter built for speed and agility above all else. These mass-produced craft form the backbone of any rebel fighter screen, darting through capital ship crossfire to deliver torpedo strikes against targets a hundred times their size. Cheap enough to build in hidden factories, fast enough to outrun anything that outguns them.
vehicles.notes: Iconic rebel workhorse. Extremely manoeuvrable but fragile — one good hit finishes the fight.

vehicles.name: Heavy Strike Fighter
vehicles.desc: Heavier than an interceptor and slower on the turn, but she carries enough ordnance to gut a frigate. Twin-seat cockpit with a dedicated weapons officer managing torpedo locks and shield balancing. The rebel answer to the question: what if we need to kill something bigger than us?
vehicles.notes: Bomber/strike role. Relies on fighter escort to survive the approach run.

vehicles.name: Converted Light Freighter
vehicles.desc: Civilian cargo hauler with the hold ripped out and gun mounts welded on. She looks like junk and handles like a shopping cart on ice, but there's enough room for a crew that feels like family, enough hidden compartments to run a blockade, and enough surprises under the hull plating to make a patrol corvette think twice about scanning her.
vehicles.notes: The classic smuggler/adventurer ship. Crew quarters for 6-8 in converted cargo space.

vehicles.name: Armed Transport
vehicles.desc: Bulk troop carrier pressed into service as a front-line warship because the rebellion fights with what it has, not what it wants. Thick hull plating protects the hundreds of soldiers packed into converted cargo bays. The gun crews are volunteers with three weeks of training and a prayer.
vehicles.notes: Can carry a full company of troops. Slow, tough, undergunned for her size.

vehicles.name: Alliance Frigate
vehicles.desc: Purpose-built warship representing the rebellion's graduation from improvised junk to genuine naval power. Fast enough to screen capital ships, well-armed enough to hunt corvettes, and carrying enough point-defence to protect troop transports from fighter swarms. The first ship the rebellion built that made the empire pay attention.

vehicles.name: Rebel Cruiser
vehicles.desc: Organic-hulled capital ship donated by an aquatic species whose shipyards grow vessels rather than build them. The curved hull disperses energy weapon impacts with unsettling efficiency, and her redundant systems mean she keeps fighting long after angular warships of similar tonnage have gone dark. Command bridge sits deep in the hull, not in an exposed tower.
vehicles.notes: Tough, well-shielded, excellent crew survival. The rebellion's first true capital ship.

vehicles.name: Blockade Buster
vehicles.desc: Stripped-down corvette built for one job: punching through orbital blockades at speed. Oversized engines bolted to an undersized hull, just enough armour to survive the sprint through the kill zone, and a cargo bay big enough to carry critical supplies to besieged worlds. Her crews age ten years on every run.
vehicles.notes: Extremely fast but fragile. Cargo capacity despite small combat profile.

vehicles.name: Veteran Gunship
vehicles.desc: Mid-size combat vessel that punches well above her weight. Originally a system patrol craft, she's been refitted so many times that nothing on board is original equipment. What she lacks in elegance she makes up for in concentrated firepower — four turrets crammed onto a hull designed for two.
vehicles.notes: Glass cannon. Heavy weapons on a medium hull. Experienced crew essential.

vehicles.name: Shuttle (Rebel)
vehicles.desc: Captured diplomatic shuttle still wearing the insignia of the government it fled. Unarmed and relying entirely on speed and a convincing transponder code to survive. Used for command transfers, VIP evacuation, and the sort of missions where getting caught means a firing squad.
vehicles.notes: Unarmed. Diplomatic clearance codes may grant passage through checkpoints.

vehicles.name: Rebel Command Ship
vehicles.desc: The largest vessel in the freedom fleet, and she bears the scars to prove it. Every battle the rebellion has fought is etched into her hull plating. She coordinates fleet actions from a cavernous combat information centre buried beneath layers of armour and redundant systems. When she jumps into a system, the empire knows the rebellion means to fight, not raid.
vehicles.notes: Fleet flagship. Extensive communications and coordination capability.
